frontierComparison.interpretation: The next-rank 13-bucket batch cover consumed its finite source token with no survivors, but emitted a larger 15 later-prime boundary. This assembly does not claim a global decrease; it consumes the immediate post-cover decision obligation by selecting a whole 15-bucket compression/ranked-boundary action before singleton q-child descent.

assembledPieces.claim: All 718 source rows and all 170,308,883,793 next q-avoiding source classes from the 13-bucket next-rank token have a later square-obstruction child by prime 199.
assembledPieces.boundary: This closes the source token as a no-survivor batch classification, but it emits successor root-child and later q-avoiding rank obligations.

assembledPieces.claim: The later square-obstruction children are partitioned into 15 later-prime buckets q in {127,131,137,139,149,151,157,163,167,173,179,191,193,197,199}, each with exactly two roots per source class.
assembledPieces.boundary: The 15 buckets are exact successor boundaries, not terminal closures.

assembledPieces.claim: The batch cover emits 340,617,767,586 later root children.
assembledPieces.boundary: Those root children remain outside the assembled proof until a later packet compresses, closes, blocks, or ranks them.

assembledPieces.claim: The same 15 later-prime buckets emit 3,652,250,197,976,151 later q-avoiding classes.
assembledPieces.boundary: Those classes require a compression theorem, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or deterministic ranked boundary before any singleton q-child descent.

supportedClaim: For the selected p443/q97/p479-available branch, the 13-bucket next-rank batch cover is consumed with no source survivors. The live successor surface is now the whole 15 later-prime rank boundary, so the next theorem step must compress or deterministically boundary that whole surface before q127, q131, or any other singleton child is opened.

compressionCandidate.name: p443_q97_p479_next_rank_later_prime_15_bucket_rank_compression
compressionCandidate.coveredFamily: All 15 later-prime buckets emitted by the next-rank 13-bucket batch cover: 718 source rows, 170,308,883,793 source next q-avoiding classes, 340,617,767,586 later root children, and 3,652,250,197,976,151 later q-avoiding classes.
compressionCandidate.whyThisCompresses: The next-rank cover already proves row-uniform first later obstruction and a shared two-root law for every stored source row. The remaining theorem-facing object is therefore the 15-prime rank surface, not any one q-child.
compressionCandidate.whyNotSingletonDescent: A q127, q131, or later singleton descent would consume only one emitted bucket while the frontier has expanded to 49 obligations. The finite token now available is the whole 15-bucket rank boundary.
compressionCandidate.failureBoundary: If no common compression theorem or impossibility theorem is visible, emit an exact deterministic 15-bucket ranked boundary packet with all buckets accounted, explicit survivors if any, and the next finite token.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.exactBoundary: The next theorem surface is the 15 later-prime bucket family: 340,617,767,586 later root children and 3,652,250,197,976,151 later q-avoiding classes.

oneNextAction.action: Compress the 15 later-prime buckets into theorem-facing families, prove a structural rank decrease or impossibility theorem, or emit an exact deterministic 15-bucket ranked boundary before singleton q-child descent.
oneNextAction.coveredFamily: All 15 later-prime buckets q in {127,131,137,139,149,151,157,163,167,173,179,191,193,197,199}, covering 340,617,767,586 later root children and 3,652,250,197,976,151 later q-avoiding classes.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: A deterministic ranked boundary packet with all 15 buckets accounted, explicit survivors if any, and the next finite token; no singleton q-child descent is opened by this assembly.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The 15 buckets share the same row-uniform two-root mechanism and are exact at the rank-token level, so a whole-boundary compression/rank packet is more informative than starting q127/q131/etc. one by one.
oneNextAction.completionRule: The next packet accounts for every one of the 15 later-prime buckets by compression theorem, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact deterministic ranked boundary.

proofBoundary: This packet is convergence assembly after the next-rank 13-bucket batch cover. It chooses a whole 15-bucket later-prime compression/ranked-boundary action and blocks singleton q-child descent. It does not close any emitted later bucket, root child, q-avoiding class, unavailable complement, p4217 complement, collision-free matching obligation, or Problem 848.
nextTheoremMove: Compress the 15 later-prime rank buckets, prove a structural/impossibility theorem over the whole later-prime surface, or emit an exact deterministic 15-bucket ranked boundary; do not open q127/q131/singleton descent first.
